features: watchdog enable, bark, bite, and pet behavior
watchdog lock behavior with reset-only unlock
reset initialization and read-only/write-only CSR semantics

directed_tests.name: reset_and_csr_sanity
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset values, TL-UL accessibility, RO/WO/W1C semantics, and basic CSR map decoding for all 15 registers within the 64-byte window.

directed_tests.name: mtime_free_run_and_prescaler_basic
directed_tests.intent: Confirm mtime starts at zero, increments monotonically, and advances at 1/(prescaler+1) rate for prescaler values 0, 1, and a mid-range value.

directed_tests.name: mtimecmp0_interrupt_basic
directed_tests.intent: Program mtimecmp0 below and above current mtime, verify raw interrupt assertion, INTR_ENABLE masking, and W1C clearing through INTR_STATE.

directed_tests.name: mtimecmp1_interrupt_basic
directed_tests.intent: Repeat comparator interrupt checks for mtimecmp1 to ensure both comparator channels operate independently.

directed_tests.name: partial_64bit_compare_write_order
directed_tests.intent: Write comparator LOW then HIGH and HIGH then LOW while mtime is active to validate intermediate states and final compare behavior without atomic write assumptions.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_test_register
directed_tests.intent: Use INTR_TEST to force each interrupt bit, confirm INTR_STATE sets, output pin follows INTR_ENABLE, and W1C clear works per bit.

directed_tests.name: watchdog_enable_bark_bite
directed_tests.intent: Enable watchdog, set bark and bite thresholds, verify bark interrupt at threshold and alert_o at bite threshold, including persistence until pet/reset as specified.

directed_tests.name: watchdog_pet_and_count_reset
directed_tests.intent: Write WATCHDOG_PET with arbitrary values to reset the watchdog counter and confirm counter restarts from zero while enabled.

directed_tests.name: watchdog_lock_and_reset_only_unlock
directed_tests.intent: Set wd_lock, verify WATCHDOG_CTRL cannot be cleared by software, WATCHDOG_PET remains writable, and full reset restores unlock capability.

directed_tests.name: prescaler_change_on_boundary
directed_tests.intent: Change PRESCALER while mtime is running and verify the new value takes effect on the next prescaler boundary without resetting the internal prescale phase.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz_smoke
random_tests.intent: Run constrained-random TL-UL reads/writes across valid CSR addresses, checking protocol stability, register mirroring, and no unexpected bus errors within the 60-minute budget.

random_tests.name: timer_watchdog_constrained_random
random_tests.intent: Randomize prescaler, comparator thresholds, watchdog thresholds, enable masks, and pet timing to explore mixed timer/watchdog interactions and interrupt/alert concurrency.

random_tests.name: partial_write_order_randomized
random_tests.intent: Randomly choose LOW/HIGH write order for 64-bit comparator programming and verify final compare values and interrupt outcomes against a Python reference model.

random_tests.name: interrupt_masking_random
random_tests.intent: Randomly toggle INTR_ENABLE while raw interrupt sources are active to validate masking behavior and W1C clearing under changing enable conditions.

risk_areas.reason: Lock is sticky until hardware reset and can easily be implemented incorrectly, especially around write filtering and pet accessibility.
Comparator registers are written as two 32-bit halves; intermediate states can cause false early/late interrupts if update ordering is mishandled.
Changing PRESCALER without resetting the internal phase is subtle and likely to expose off-by-one or phase-alignment bugs.
W1C, masking, and software-forced test bits must combine correctly to drive intr_o without corrupting raw state.
Threshold comparison and concurrent alert/interrupt behavior are timing-sensitive and may differ from the intended model.
Read-only mtime and write-only WATCHDOG_PET should ignore illegal access patterns cleanly; these are common integration issues.

risk_areas.area: TL-UL register decode and address alignment
risk_areas.reason: A 64-byte window with 15 CSRs is small, but decode mistakes can silently break coverage and software access.
